place: Edwards CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: Edwards Historical Association was founded in 1982 and is located in Edwards, New York.
The museum's new building has expanded the collection of historical material and artifacts that concern the history of the Town of Edwards.
The Edwards Historical Association also produces a quarterly newsletter and publishes books about the local history.
Farming has been a major factor in this town and for over 200 years where milk, cheese, butter, maple syrup, pork and beef were vital.
The town was organized by legislature on April 7, 1827, after settlers arrived in 1812 and a Scottish influx in 1819.
The Edwards Historical Association collects and preserves artifacts and memories of Edwards, then shares them through museum visits, meetings, and cultural events.
